edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:e9f87d9199c2
edges.original_sentence: By 1925, overfishing and the removal of more than 46 million m3 of substrates from the Detroit River (Bennion & Manny2011), and a like amount of substrates from the St. Clair River, to create deep-draft shipping channels, greatly reduced the amount of spawning habitat and fish populations in these rivers.

source_spans.text: By 1925, overfishing and the removal of more than 46 million m3 of substrates from the Detroit River (Bennion & Manny2011), and a like amount of substrates from the St. Clair River, to create deep-draft shipping channels, greatly reduced the amount of spawning habitat and fish populations in these rivers.

source_document.title: A scientific basis for restoring fish spawning habitat in the St. Clair and Detroit Rivers of the Laurentian Great Lakes

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:e9f87d9199c2
edges.original_sentence: By 1925, overfishing and the removal of more than 46 million m3 of substrates from the Detroit River (Bennion & Manny2011), and a like amount of substrates from the St. Clair River, to create deep-draft shipping channels, greatly reduced the amount of spawning habitat and fish populations in these rivers.

source_spans.text: By 1925, overfishing and the removal of more than 46 million m3 of substrates from the Detroit River (Bennion & Manny2011), and a like amount of substrates from the St. Clair River, to create deep-draft shipping channels, greatly reduced the amount of spawning habitat and fish populations in these rivers.

source_document.title: A scientific basis for restoring fish spawning habitat in the St. Clair and Detroit Rivers of the Laurentian Great Lakes

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:8fee7e0581f5
edges.original_sentence: Particularly damaging was the Livingstone Channel project, in the early 1900s. This 19.3km shipping channel was cut through a limestone bedrock sill near themouthoftheDetroitRiver,withaminimumwidthanddepth of 244m and 6.4m, respectively, and removed 46,200,000m3 of substrate (Bennion & Manny 2011). The Livingstone Channel project greatly altered the river’s hydrology, destroyed many hectares of fish spawning grounds in the river (Manny et al. 1988), and coincided with cessation of spawning by the economically valuable C. clupeaformis in the Detroit River (Bennion & Manny 2011).

source_spans.text: Particularly damaging was the Livingstone Channel project, in the early 1900s. This 19.3km shipping channel was cut through a limestone bedrock sill near themouthoftheDetroitRiver,withaminimumwidthanddepth of 244m and 6.4m, respectively, and removed 46,200,000m3 of substrate (Bennion & Manny 2011). The Livingstone Channel project greatly altered the river’s hydrology, destroyed many hectares of fish spawning grounds in the river (Manny et al. 1988), and coincided with cessation of spawning by the economically valuable C. clupeaformis in the Detroit River (Bennion & Manny 2011).

source_document.title: A scientific basis for restoring fish spawning habitat in the St. Clair and Detroit Rivers of the Laurentian Great Lakes

edges.original_sentence: Particularly damaging was the Livingstone Channel project, in the early 1900s. This 19.3km shipping channel was cut through a limestone bedrock sill near themouthoftheDetroitRiver,withaminimumwidthanddepth of 244m and 6.4m, respectively, and removed 46,200,000m3 of substrate (Bennion & Manny 2011). The Livingstone Channel project greatly altered the river’s hydrology, destroyed many hectares of fish spawning grounds in the river (Manny et al. 1988), and coincided with cessation of spawning by the economically valuable C. clupeaformis in the Detroit River (Bennion & Manny 2011).

source_spans.text: Particularly damaging was the Livingstone Channel project, in the early 1900s. This 19.3km shipping channel was cut through a limestone bedrock sill near themouthoftheDetroitRiver,withaminimumwidthanddepth of 244m and 6.4m, respectively, and removed 46,200,000m3 of substrate (Bennion & Manny 2011). The Livingstone Channel project greatly altered the river’s hydrology, destroyed many hectares of fish spawning grounds in the river (Manny et al. 1988), and coincided with cessation of spawning by the economically valuable C. clupeaformis in the Detroit River (Bennion & Manny 2011).

source_document.title: A scientific basis for restoring fish spawning habitat in the St. Clair and Detroit Rivers of the Laurentian Great Lakes

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:d7bd250b5441
edges.original_sentence: Sediment infilling of some of the spawning reef beds constructed at the Fighting Island and Middle Channel projects taught us that small differences in reef placement within a channel can influence infilling with sediment and reef performance.

source_spans.text: Sediment infilling of some of the spawning reef beds constructed at the Fighting Island and Middle Channel projects taught us that small differences in reef placement within a channel can influence infilling with sediment and reef performance.

source_document.title: A scientific basis for restoring fish spawning habitat in the St. Clair and Detroit Rivers of the Laurentian Great Lakes

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:f59b27efd826
edges.original_sentence: Sites selected for future reef restoration have lower potential for infilling, due to the team’s ability to identify and avoid depositional zones as a result of this consultation.

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:a7a17cb714f0
edges.original_sentence: Siltation and removal of fish spawning habitats, altered hydrological conditions disconnecting floodplains from river channels, and water pollution have reduced the reproductive capacity of large river systems (Berkman & Rabini 1987; Mion et al. 1998; Humphries & Lake 2000).

source_spans.text: Siltation and removal of fish spawning habitats, altered hydrological conditions disconnecting floodplains from river channels, and water pollution have reduced the reproductive capacity of large river systems (Berkman & Rabini 1987; Mion et al. 1998; Humphries & Lake 2000).

source_document.title: A scientific basis for restoring fish spawning habitat in the St. Clair and Detroit Rivers of the Laurentian Great Lakes

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:aac6b2248e41
edges.original_sentence: Without complete knowledge of physical habitat and biological requirements of target species during their spawning period in the river, success in constructing long-lasting, productive habitat will be limited.

source_spans.text: Without complete knowledge of physical habitat and biological requirements of target species during their spawning period in the river, success in constructing long-lasting, productive habitat will be limited.
